note: Separate from the curated -reference.json; merge in when wiring the single-file consumer.

questions.stem: Woman: Didn't I just see you in the library an hour ago?

options.A: As a matter of fact, I was returning a book.
options.B: Yes, you can find it in the reference section.
options.C: I don't think I'll have enough time to do that.
options.D: Actually, I think I can get there a little earlier.

questions.stem: Man: Where is the nearest bus stop?

options.C: I can help you find it.

questions.stem: Woman: How do I contact customer service?

options.A: Yes, you're allowed to do that.
options.C: No, I don't mind.

questions.stem: Woman: I'm afraid I'm not available this evening.

options.C: She arrived this afternoon.

questions.stem: Man: Isn't the post office open today?

options.C: I think he's come home already.

questions.stem: Woman: If you need me, just text.

options.A: I can help you with that.
options.B: You don't need any more information.
options.C: You have a lot of questions, don't you?
options.D: You haven't given me your number yet.

questions.stem: Woman: So the store is open for business all weekend?

options.A: Yes, there is a major power outage.
options.C: Yes, it's closed all day on Sunday.
options.D: Yes, they're having a huge sale.

questions.stem: Man: Did you attend the seminar?

options.C: Have you asked your professor?

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Need anything from the supermarket?

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Huh? Aren't we getting ready to go see that play in a few minutes?

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: That's tomorrow.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Oh. Wow, I'd forget my head if it wasn't screwed on.... Guess I don't need to change my clothes after all.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: So, you weren't planning to prepare dinner?

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: No, but I can. What do you want?

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Just something light and healthy. So, can you go shopping instead?

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Yeah, sure. How about salmon and salad? Want anything else?

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: No, that's good. Thanks!

questions.stem: What does the woman imply that she was about to do?

options.C: Go shopping

questions.stem: Why does the man say "I'd forget my head if it wasn't screwed on"?

options.A: He forgot what the woman wanted him to buy.
options.B: He forgot about the timing of their plans.
options.C: He forgot what they were going to eat for dinner.
options.D: He forgot to buy salmon and salad at the supermarket.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Did you see the maintenance request about the air-conditioning?

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Yes, I called the technician this morning. They said someone should be here shortly.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: That's a relief. It's getting uncomfortably warm in here.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: I know--I called as soon as I noticed. Hopefully, it's just a minor issue and they can get it working again without too much delay. In the meantime, why don't you go out to lunch early? Maybe it'll be better when you get back.

questions.stem: Why did the woman call a technician?

options.C: An elevator needs maintenance.

questions.stem: What does the woman suggest the man do?

options.C: Open a door

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: I'm trying to decide whether to get a smartphone or a tablet. What do you think?

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: It depends on what you need it for. If you want something portable and easy to use on the go, a smartphone is better.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: That's true. But I like the larger screen on a tablet for watching videos and reading.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: In that case....

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: I better think about it some more.

questions.stem: What is the man trying to decide between?

options.C: A tablet and a desktop computer

questions.stem: What reason does the woman give for her suggestion?

options.B: The device is easier to use on the go.
options.C: The device has a larger screen.
options.D: The device has better battery life.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: I think my laptop is acting up again. It keeps freezing and shutting down randomly.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: That's frustrating. Have you tried restarting it or running a virus scan?

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Yes, I did both, but it didn't help. I might need to take it to a repair shop.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: That sounds like a good idea. They should be able to diagnose and fix the issue.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: I'll do that this afternoon.

questions.stem: What problem is the man experiencing with his laptop?

options.C: It keeps freezing and shutting down.

questions.stem: What will the man most likely do next?

options.C: Take his laptop to a repair shop

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Are you going to the supermarket today by any chance?

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: I was just there yesterday. Why?

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: I suddenly remembered that I'm supposed to bring some drinks to my team's lunch meeting tomorrow at work, and I'm in the middle of a project I need to finish tonight.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: You're in luck. I stocked up yesterday.

questions.stem: What is the man's problem?

options.A: He has nothing to eat for lunch.
options.B: He is unable to attend a meeting.
options.C: He forgot about an obligation at work.
options.D: He needs more resources to finish a project.

questions.stem: What does the woman imply when she says, "I stocked up yesterday"?

options.A: She does not plan to go to work tomorrow.
options.B: She will provide the man with drinks for his meeting.
options.C: The supermarket has plenty of drinks available.
options.D: The man will not need extra time to finish his project.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Have you returned the books we borrowed from the library?

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Why? I thought we could keep them for six weeks.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Actually, it's now four weeks. They changed the policy. I guess I forgot to tell you!

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: That means they're due tomorrow!

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Exactly!

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: In that case, I'll take care of it right now!

questions.stem: What was the woman not aware of?

options.C: The library's holiday schedule

questions.stem: What will the woman probably do next?

options.B: Make a phone call to the library
options.C: Pay a fine for returning books late
options.D: Borrow a new book from the library
